Container.Name: OpenScrub
Container.Overview: Local video redaction with human review — nothing leaves your server. Detects and blurs faces and any on-screen text (names, SSNs, phones, emails, addresses, card numbers, license plates, custom regex categories) in videos and screen recordings. OCR-driven with scroll tracking for screen captures and automatic camera-footage handling; review every detection in the web UI before rendering; optional at-rest encryption of the job store; audit reports with SHA-256 provenance. This is the CPU image — for NVIDIA-accelerated OCR and NVENC encoding install the OpenScrub-CUDA template instead. Best-effort redaction: always review output before sharing.

Config.Name: WebUI port
Config.Description: HTTPS web interface (self-signed certificate — your browser warns once, or install your own cert in App settings)

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Jobs (contains the sensitive uploads and reports!), certificates, zones, downloaded models, encryption vault. Everything OpenScrub stores lives here — the container itself is disposable.

Config.Name: Access token (optional)
Config.Description: Leave empty for open access on your LAN, or set a secret to require it on every request (recommended if others use your network). The WebUI URL becomes https://ip:8384/?token=SECRET on first visit.
